stories.title: Qilin Ransomware Attackers Exploit PAN-OS Authentication Bypass for Initial Access
stories.abstract: Qilin ransomware operators are actively leveraging a patched authentication bypass vulnerability in Palo Alto Networks' PAN-OS. The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-0257, allows unauthorized access to GlobalProtect portals and gateways, serving as the initial entry point for subsequent ransomware deployment.
stories.why: This vulnerability provides a direct path for ransomware actors to bypass authentication on perimeter security appliances, facilitating network-wide compromise.
stories.action: Ensure all PAN-OS instances are updated to the latest patched version.

stories.title: Mozilla security advisory (AV26-726)
stories.abstract: Mozilla has released critical patches for Firefox and Firefox ESR to address multiple vulnerabilities. Simultaneously, CISA has added several of these flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, confirming active exploitation in the wild.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities are confirmed to be under active exploitation, posing a significant risk of remote code execution or system compromise.
stories.action: Update Firefox to 153, Firefox ESR to 140.13 or 115.38, and patch affected Fortinet appliances immediately.

stories.title: WordPress wp2shell Exploitation Grows as Public Exploit Fuels Mass Scanning
stories.abstract: Threat actors are actively chaining two critical vulnerabilities, CVE-2026-60137 and CVE-2026-63030, to achieve unauthenticated remote code execution on WordPress sites. Publicly available exploit code has triggered widespread automated scanning and compromise attempts against the platform.
stories.why: This exploit chain allows full site takeover, posing a severe risk to the integrity and confidentiality of any unpatched WordPress installation.
stories.action: Update WordPress to version 7.0.2, 6.9.5, or 6.8.6 immediately.

stories.title: Critical ServiceNow AI Platform Flaw Exploited for Unauthenticated Code Execution
stories.abstract: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical sandbox escape vulnerability, CVE-2026-6875, within the ServiceNow AI platform. This flaw allows unauthenticated remote code execution, prompting urgent warnings from security agencies following reports of in-the-wild attacks.
stories.why: Unauthenticated RCE in a widely used enterprise platform poses a severe risk of full system compromise and lateral movement.
stories.action: Apply the latest ServiceNow patches for Brazil, Australia, Zurich, and Yokohama versions immediately.

stories.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
stories.abstract: Threat actors exploited two zero-day vulnerabilities in SonicWall SMA 1000 series VPN appliances to achieve root-level access. The campaign, attributed to a group dubbed UTA0533, involved deploying custom malware to compromise the devices starting in late June.
stories.why: VPN appliances are high-value targets for initial access; root-level compromise allows attackers to bypass authentication and maintain persistent network presence.
stories.action: Apply the latest security patches provided by SonicWall for SMA 1000 series appliances immediately.

stories.title: Estée Lauder discloses data breach via Oracle E-Business flaw
stories.abstract: Estée Lauder confirmed that attackers exploited a zero-day vulnerability in its Oracle E-Business Suite to access sensitive HR data. The breach resulted in the exfiltration of personal, financial, and health records belonging to individuals within the company's systems.
stories.why: Exploitation of zero-day vulnerabilities in enterprise resource planning software highlights significant risks to internal HR and sensitive employee data repositories.

stories.title: Windows LegacyHive zero-day flaw gets free, unofficial patches
stories.abstract: An independent security researcher released unofficial patches for a Windows zero-day vulnerability that enables local privilege escalation. The flaw affects current versions of the operating system, and Microsoft has not yet provided an official fix.
stories.why: Unpatched privilege escalation vulnerabilities allow attackers to gain administrative control over compromised systems, significantly increasing the impact of initial access.

stories.title: AI music platform Suno hits bum note as 55M users exposed in data breach, claims infosec expert
stories.abstract: AI music platform Suno suffered a data breach exposing the personal information of approximately 55 million users. Compromised records include names, phone numbers, and physical addresses, as confirmed by the Have I Been Pwned service.
stories.why: Large-scale credential and PII leaks facilitate targeted phishing campaigns and identity theft against the affected user base.

stories.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: WordPress RCE, SonicWall 0-Days, AI Service Attacks, SharePoint 0-Day and More
stories.abstract: Recent security disclosures highlight a surge in critical vulnerabilities across enterprise infrastructure, including remote code execution flaws in WordPress and SonicWall devices. Attackers are actively exploiting these gaps, alongside SharePoint weaknesses, to bypass security controls and compromise sensitive system data.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities provide direct entry points for attackers to gain unauthorized system access, exfiltrate data, or deploy persistent malware within enterprise environments.
stories.action: Audit and patch all internet-facing SonicWall, WordPress, and SharePoint instances immediately.

stories.title: New HollowGraph malware uses Microsoft Graph for stealthy C2 comms
stories.abstract: The HollowGraph malware leverages Microsoft Graph APIs and compromised Microsoft 365 calendar entries to establish covert command-and-control channels. This technique allows attackers to exfiltrate data and issue instructions while blending in with legitimate cloud-based traffic.
stories.why: It demonstrates a sophisticated method for bypassing traditional network perimeter defenses by abusing trusted enterprise SaaS infrastructure for malicious communication.

stories.title: New Orleans Cops Published Policy Document Allowing Weaponized Drones
stories.abstract: New Orleans police department documentation reveals the agency has established formal policies regarding the potential deployment of weaponized unmanned aerial vehicles, signaling a shift toward integrating lethal capabilities into municipal law enforcement drone programs.
stories.why: This indicates a significant escalation in domestic surveillance and tactical capabilities, raising major civil liberty and oversight concerns for security professionals.

stories.title: Microsoft security advisory – July 2026 monthly rollup (AV26-698) – Update 2
stories.abstract: Microsoft released a comprehensive set of security updates covering its .NET ecosystem, various Azure services, Microsoft 365 applications, and GitHub Copilot plugins. These patches address multiple vulnerabilities across Windows, Linux, and macOS environments to mitigate potential exploitation risks.
stories.why: Widespread vulnerabilities in core development frameworks and cloud infrastructure can facilitate remote code execution or unauthorized access across enterprise environments.
stories.action: Apply the July 2026 security updates to all affected .NET, Azure, and Microsoft 365 deployments.

stories.title: Zyxel security advisory (AV26-725)
stories.abstract: Zyxel has released security updates for various DSL/Ethernet CPE, Fiber ONT, and wireless extender models. These patches remediate a post-authentication command injection vulnerability that could allow an attacker with valid credentials to execute unauthorized commands on affected network hardware.
stories.why: Command injection vulnerabilities in network infrastructure allow attackers to gain persistent control over edge devices, facilitating lateral movement and network compromise.
stories.action: Review Zyxel's advisory and apply the latest firmware updates to all affected CPE, ONT, and extender devices.

stories.title: Tenable security advisory (AV26-724)
stories.abstract: Security advisories were issued for Tenable Security Center (versions 6.6.0-6.8.0), HPE Telco Automated Assurance (v1.4 and earlier), and Zimbra Collaboration Suite Classic Web Client (versions before 10.1.20) to address various vulnerabilities.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities could allow unauthorized access or system compromise if left unpatched in enterprise environments.
stories.action: Apply patch SC202607.1 for Tenable, update HPE Telco Automated Assurance, and upgrade Zimbra to 10.1.20.

stories.title: Closing the Identity Gaps in Critical Infrastructure Security
stories.abstract: Security researchers are highlighting the persistent risk of identity-based attacks against critical infrastructure. By leveraging stolen credentials and compromised endpoints, adversaries frequently bypass traditional perimeter defenses, necessitating a shift toward rigorous verification of both user identity and device health.
stories.why: Identity remains the primary attack vector for industrial control systems, making robust authentication and device posture checks essential for preventing unauthorized lateral movement.

stories.title: New ENCFORGE Ransomware Targets AI Model Files in Langflow RCE Attack
stories.abstract: Threat actors are exploiting remote code execution vulnerabilities in Langflow servers to deploy a new Go-based ransomware strain called ENCFORGE. This malware specifically targets AI infrastructure, including model weights, training data, and vector indexes, to disrupt machine learning operations.
stories.why: The emergence of ransomware specifically designed to encrypt AI model assets highlights a growing trend of targeting specialized machine learning infrastructure.

stories.title: World's Largest AI Model Repository Hugging Face Breached by Autonomous AI Agent
stories.abstract: Hugging Face reported a security breach involving unauthorized access to internal datasets and credentials. The company confirmed that the intrusion was executed by an autonomous AI agent targeting their production infrastructure, though the scope of the compromised data remains limited.
stories.why: This incident highlights the emerging risk of autonomous agents being weaponized to exploit infrastructure, necessitating new defensive strategies for AI-driven development environments.
stories.action: Rotate all credentials and API keys associated with Hugging Face production environments.

stories.title: Zimbra Patches Critical SNMP Command Injection and Four XSS Vulnerabilities
stories.abstract: Zimbra released updates for version 10.1.20 to resolve nine security flaws. The most severe issue is a command injection vulnerability within the SNMP monitoring component, alongside various cross-site scripting, server-side request forgery, and restriction bypass defects.
stories.why: Command injection vulnerabilities in network management components allow unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code with elevated privileges on the underlying server.
stories.action: Update Zimbra to version 10.1.20 immediately.

stories.title: Clover Health Investments Discloses Data Breach
stories.abstract: Clover Health Investments confirmed a data breach resulting from a social engineering attack. Unauthorized actors gained access to employee accounts, potentially exposing sensitive personal and health-related information belonging to the company's clients.
stories.why: Credential compromise via social engineering remains a primary vector for unauthorized access to sensitive healthcare data and PII.

stories.title: A Device Hidden in Cars Across the US Leaves Them Vulnerable to Hacking and Paralysis. Patch It Now
stories.abstract: Millions of vehicles across the United States contain aftermarket alarm systems installed by dealerships that remain active even when not purchased. Researchers discovered these devices possess critical vulnerabilities allowing unauthorized parties to remotely track, unlock, and disable the affected automobiles.
stories.why: These insecure, persistent third-party devices create a massive, unmanaged attack surface that bypasses factory-grade automotive security controls.
stories.action: Identify and physically disconnect unauthorized aftermarket alarm hardware from your vehicle's wiring harness.

stories.title: Captive Portal Detection, (Tue, Jul 21st)
stories.abstract: SANS researchers identified unexpected traffic directed toward a Firefox captive portal detection URL. This activity, while appearing benign, highlights how automated browser mechanisms can generate network noise that might be misidentified as malicious scanning or reconnaissance by security monitoring systems.
stories.why: Security teams must distinguish between legitimate browser-based connectivity checks and actual malicious probes to reduce false positive alerts in network logs.

stories.title: Kenya probes hack of president's website after bitcoin ransom demand
stories.abstract: The official website for Kenya's president was compromised over the weekend. Attackers defaced the homepage, demanding a cryptocurrency payment and threatening to leak sensitive data regarding President William Ruto if their financial demands were not met.
stories.why: This incident highlights the ongoing risk of high-profile government web defacement and extortion attempts targeting political leadership.

stories.title: New HollowGraph Malware Abuses Microsoft 365 Calendar for C&C Communication
stories.abstract: Attackers are utilizing compromised Microsoft 365 accounts to facilitate command-and-control operations. By leveraging calendar entries as a dead-drop mechanism, the HollowGraph malware establishes a covert, two-way communication channel that blends in with legitimate enterprise traffic.
stories.why: This technique bypasses traditional network-based detection by abusing trusted SaaS infrastructure for malicious signaling.

stories.title: JadePuffer agentic attacks now target AI model data with ransomware
stories.abstract: The JadePuffer autonomous agent has integrated EncForge, a specialized malware strain designed to target and encrypt critical AI infrastructure. This threat specifically focuses on locking up training datasets, vector databases, and model checkpoints to extort organizations.
stories.why: This represents an emerging class of ransomware specifically targeting the integrity and availability of AI model development pipelines.

stories.title: Apps Marketed to US Troops Are Shipping Chinese and Russian Code
stories.abstract: A recent investigation revealed that over 12% of mobile applications marketed to American military personnel integrate software components originating from foreign nations, including countries officially classified by the Department of Defense as strategic adversaries.
stories.why: Foreign-sourced code in military-targeted software introduces significant supply chain risks, potential data exfiltration vectors, and unauthorized surveillance capabilities against sensitive personnel.

stories.title: More than 1,000 domains illegally streaming World Cup games seized, DOJ says
stories.abstract: The U.S. Department of Justice has shut down over 1,000 websites and blocked nearly 2,000 domains that were illegally broadcasting FIFA World Cup matches. The operation targeted unauthorized streaming services to protect intellectual property rights during the tournament.
stories.why: Large-scale domain seizures highlight ongoing federal efforts to disrupt illicit content distribution networks and the infrastructure supporting digital piracy.

stories.title: New 7-Zip Vulnerability Could Let Crafted XZ Archives Run Code During Extraction
stories.abstract: A heap-based buffer overflow vulnerability in 7-Zip, tracked as CVE-2026-14266, allows remote code execution when processing malicious XZ archives. The flaw stems from improper handling of chunked data during the extraction process.
stories.why: Arbitrary code execution via common archive formats poses a significant risk to endpoint integrity and facilitates initial access for attackers.
stories.action: Update 7-Zip to version 26.02 or later.

stories.title: Red Hat security advisory (AV26-719)
stories.abstract: Red Hat released a series of security patches between July 13 and July 19, 2026, targeting multiple vulnerabilities within the Linux kernel across several Enterprise Linux distributions, including CodeReady Linux Builder and Real Time editions.
stories.why: Kernel-level vulnerabilities often provide local privilege escalation vectors, necessitating prompt remediation to maintain system integrity and defense-in-depth.
stories.action: Review Red Hat security advisories and apply kernel updates to affected systems.

stories.title: Ubuntu security advisory (AV26-717)
stories.abstract: Ubuntu has released security patches for multiple kernel vulnerabilities impacting several versions of the operating system, including 14.04, 16.04, 20.04, 24.04, and 25.10. Administrators should review the official security notices to identify the specific fixes required for their environments.
stories.why: Kernel-level vulnerabilities often provide local privilege escalation paths or system instability, necessitating prompt remediation in production environments.
stories.action: Review Ubuntu Security Notices and apply kernel updates to affected systems.

stories.title: A New Ransomware Threat Actor Emerges Every Week, Warns Report
stories.abstract: A recent analysis indicates that the ransomware landscape is experiencing rapid expansion and increased fragmentation, with new threat actors emerging on a weekly basis.
stories.why: Increased fragmentation complicates attribution and defense, as security teams must track a growing number of distinct, smaller groups rather than a few giants.

stories.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, July 21st, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/10016, (Tue, Jul 21st)
stories.abstract: The provided source material for the July 21st, 2026, SANS ISC Stormcast podcast is empty, offering no specific details regarding current security threats, vulnerabilities, or industry developments to summarize.

stories.title: GitHub security advisory (AV26-720)
stories.abstract: GitHub has released security updates for multiple versions of its Enterprise Server platform. These patches address vulnerabilities across the 3.17 through 3.21 release lines, requiring administrators to upgrade their instances to the latest specified versions to mitigate potential security risks.
stories.why: Unpatched enterprise server instances are high-value targets for attackers seeking to compromise source code, CI/CD pipelines, and internal development credentials.
stories.action: Update GitHub Enterprise Server to versions 3.21.3, 3.20.5, 3.19.9, 3.18.12, or 3.17.18.

stories.title: [Control systems] CISA ICS security advisories (AV26-718)
stories.abstract: CISA released multiple industrial control system advisories covering vulnerabilities in hardware and software from ABB, AutomationDirect, NASA, and Rockwell Automation. These updates address various security flaws identified across several versions of these critical infrastructure and automation products.
stories.why: Unpatched vulnerabilities in industrial control systems can lead to unauthorized access, operational disruption, or remote code execution within sensitive production environments.
stories.action: Review CISA advisory AV26-718 and apply vendor-specific patches for affected ABB, AutomationDirect, NASA, and Rockwell Automation components.

stories.title: Dell security advisory (AV26-716)
stories.abstract: Dell released a series of security updates addressing vulnerabilities across its product ecosystem, including iDRAC9, PowerEdge servers, PowerProtect Data Manager, SmartFabric Manager, Storage Monitoring and Resource Manager, and ThinOS 10. Administrators should review the latest advisories for specific version requirements.
stories.why: Unpatched infrastructure components like iDRAC and storage management tools are frequent targets for privilege escalation and remote code execution.
stories.action: Review Dell security advisories and update affected firmware and software to the specified versions.

stories.title: IBM security advisory (AV26-715)
stories.abstract: IBM released a series of security advisories covering a broad range of enterprise software, including API Connect, Cloud Object Storage, Guardium, and various CICS Transaction Gateway products. These updates address multiple vulnerabilities identified across these platforms throughout mid-July 2026.
stories.why: Unpatched enterprise infrastructure components are frequent targets for initial access and lateral movement within corporate networks.
stories.action: Review IBM security advisories and apply patches for your specific product versions.

stories.title: An AI Botnet
stories.abstract: The provided source material contains promotional links and general cybersecurity resources rather than specific details regarding a new AI-driven botnet. Consequently, there is insufficient information to describe any actual security incident or technical development.

stories.title: Russian-Speaking Hacker Uses Google Gemini CLI to Control Botnet of Eight Dental Clinic PCs
stories.abstract: A threat actor identified as bandcampro leveraged Google's Gemini CLI tool to automate botnet management and credential cracking. Analysis of session logs confirms the attacker utilized AI capabilities to maintain control over compromised dental clinic systems.
stories.why: This demonstrates how attackers are integrating LLM-based CLI tools to streamline malicious operations and lower the barrier for managing botnet infrastructure.

stories.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
stories.abstract: CISA has added a critical deserialization vulnerability in Microsoft SharePoint Server, tracked as CVE-2026-58644, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The flaw carries a CVSS score of 9.8 and is currently being leveraged in active attacks.
stories.why: This critical remote code execution vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to compromise SharePoint servers, posing a severe risk to internal network infrastructure.
stories.action: Apply the latest Microsoft security updates for SharePoint Server immediately.

stories.title: Hackers stole ‘significant’ amount of data from tech firm relied on by thousands of US hospitals and pharmacies
stories.abstract: Craneware, a software provider for thousands of American healthcare facilities, confirmed a cyberattack resulted in the theft of sensitive customer information. The breach potentially compromises billing and health-related data across numerous hospitals, clinics, and pharmacies.
stories.why: This incident highlights significant supply chain risk for the healthcare sector, potentially exposing massive amounts of sensitive patient health information.

stories.title: Critical NGINX Vulnerability Can Crash Workers and May Allow Remote Code Execution
stories.abstract: F5 released patches for a critical heap buffer overflow vulnerability in NGINX. Unauthenticated remote attackers can exploit this flaw using specially crafted HTTP requests to crash worker processes, potentially leading to remote code execution or denial of service.
stories.why: This vulnerability affects core web infrastructure, allowing attackers to disrupt service availability or potentially gain unauthorized control over the underlying server process.
stories.action: Update NGINX to 1.30.4, 1.31.3, or NGINX Plus 37.0.3.1 immediately.

stories.title: HollowGraph Malware Hides C2 and Stolen Files in Microsoft 365 Events Dated 2050
stories.abstract: The HollowGraph malware leverages the Microsoft Graph API to facilitate espionage. By creating calendar events set for the year 2050, attackers hide command-and-control instructions and exfiltrated data within legitimate Microsoft 365 traffic, effectively masking malicious activity as standard enterprise operations.
stories.why: This technique bypasses traditional network monitoring by blending malicious traffic with trusted Microsoft 365 API calls, complicating detection for security teams.

stories.title: Microsoft Exchange Hacked, Five Years Later
stories.abstract: Security researchers are finally disclosing internal details regarding the 2021 HAFNIUM campaign against Microsoft Exchange. The upcoming presentation will reveal how the group compromised tens of thousands of organizations, contrasting the initial public narrative that the attacks were limited in scope.
stories.why: Understanding the operational tradecraft of state-sponsored actors provides critical context for incident response and threat modeling against similar high-impact server vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Pay up or not? Ransomware surge has victims facing tough choices.
stories.abstract: Policymakers are debating whether to prohibit organizations from paying ransoms to cybercriminals. This legislative push aims to curb the rising frequency and complexity of extortion attacks by removing the financial incentive for threat actors.
stories.why: A ban would fundamentally alter incident response strategies, forcing organizations to prioritize resilient backups and recovery over negotiation.

stories.title: N-day is Becoming N-Hour. Patching Faster Won't Save You.
stories.abstract: Security researchers highlight the shrinking window between software patch releases and the development of functional exploits. Attackers now routinely reverse-engineer vendor updates to identify vulnerabilities, turning disclosed fixes into active threats against unpatched systems almost immediately.
stories.why: The rapid weaponization of patch diffs renders traditional patching cycles insufficient, forcing organizations to prioritize exploitability over simple vulnerability severity.

stories.title: New Bit2Watt Attack Could Let Cloud Tenants Disrupt Power Grids Without an Exploit
stories.abstract: Researchers have identified a theoretical side-channel technique called Bit2Watt, where cloud tenants manipulate GPU workloads to induce rapid power consumption fluctuations. These oscillations could potentially destabilize electrical grids, all without requiring traditional software exploits or unauthorized system access.
stories.why: This highlights a novel physical security risk where cloud infrastructure can be weaponized to cause real-world kinetic damage to power distribution networks.

stories.title: Exposed Server Reveals AI-Assisted Phishing Toolkit Behind WebDAV Malware Campaign
stories.abstract: Researchers discovered an exposed server containing a comprehensive phishing toolkit used to target Windows users. The repository included malware droppers, lure templates, and infrastructure for a live campaign masquerading as a Mexican government portal to distribute information-stealing software via WebDAV.
stories.why: The discovery highlights how threat actors are centralizing and refining automated phishing infrastructure to improve the efficacy of credential and data theft campaigns.

stories.title: Hackers steal $23.7 million in crypto from Ostium in off-chain attack
stories.abstract: The Ostium trading platform suffered a $23.7 million theft after attackers compromised the off-chain infrastructure responsible for providing price data to the protocol. The breach allowed unauthorized access to the platform's liquidity provider vault.
stories.why: This incident highlights the critical security risks associated with off-chain oracles and the potential for price manipulation to drain decentralized finance liquidity pools.

stories.title: A touchscreen and light make the new X4 Pro the best version of Xteink’s tiny e-readers
JadePuffer Returns With Ransomware Designed to Wipe AI Models
AI Companies Are Buying Tons of Old Books Because They're Free of AI Slop
Project Golden Eagle, Grok's Privacy Nightmare, and the 570-Vulnerability Patch Tuesday
Hackers are exploiting recently patched WordPress bugs, putting millions of websites at risk
Open-Source Android AI Agents Could Let Invisible Screen Text Run Code on Host PCs
Choose Wisely: AI-Generated Coding Risk Varies, A Lot
Intel fortifies Foundry with an actual customer: Fortinet
Taiwan to slow mobile data during national resilience drills
MIT to Become Hotbed of AI Video Surveillance
Russian Hacker Turns Jailbroken Claude Into Pentest Platform
Microsoft shares manual fix for WSUS sync delays and timeouts
Meta Paid $78,000 Bounty for Vulnerability Exposing Customer Support Data
